title: Penicillin
text: Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928 at St Mary's Hospital in London. The finding later led to the development of antibiotics.

title: Japan
text: The capital city of Japan is Tokyo. Tokyo is one of the largest metropolitan areas in the world.

title: iPhone
text: Apple announced the first iPhone in 2007. The launch changed the smartphone industry.

title: Mars
text: Mars is known as the Red Planet because of iron oxide on its surface.

title: Canada
text: Ottawa is the capital city of Canada. Toronto is the largest city in Canada by population.

title: Python Language
text: Python is a high level programming language created by Guido van Rossum. The first public release was in 1991.

title: World War II
text: World War II ended in 1945. The war involved major powers across multiple continents.

title: Mona Lisa
text: The Mona Lisa was painted by Leonardo da Vinci. It is displayed in the Louvre Museum in Paris.

title: Periodic Table
text: Dmitri Mendeleev is often credited with creating the first widely recognized periodic table in 1869.

title: Mount Everest
text: Mount Everest is the highest mountain above sea level. It lies in the Himalayas on the border between Nepal and China.

title: Water Boiling Point
text: At standard atmospheric pressure, water boils at 100 degrees Celsius.

title: Light Speed
text: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometers per second.

title: United Nations
text: The United Nations was founded in 1945 after World War II to promote international cooperation.

title: The Great Wall
text: The Great Wall of China is a series of fortifications built across northern China over many dynasties.

title: Amazon River
text: The Amazon River is one of the longest rivers in the world and has the largest drainage basin.

title: Nobel Prize
text: Alfred Nobel established the Nobel Prizes in his will. The prizes were first awarded in 1901.

title: DNA
text: DNA stands for deoxyribonucleic acid. The double helix structure was described by James Watson and Francis Crick in 1953.

title: Internet
text: The ARPANET project in the late 1960s is considered a key precursor to the modern internet.

title: Basketball
text: Basketball was invented by James Naismith in 1891.

title: Moon Landing
text: Apollo 11 landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Neil Armstrong became the first person to walk on the lunar surface.
